description: People 'manawi' when they reach an agreement by each accepting less than they first sought. The settlement may preserve cooperation, while justice and freedom remain questions of their own. A result reached under pressure is still open to refusal and review.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and open onto central 'a'. Set the tongue tip at the upper teeth for stressed voiced dental nasal 'n' and repeat the open central vowel. Round the lips for voiced 'w', then narrow into high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The first two syllables repeat open 'a' through nasal contacts made at different places, lips first and teeth second. Final 'wi' joins those steady openings to a narrower glide, so the word changes course without losing its shared resonance.
usage_notes: The verb takes plural participants or a comitative phrase; the event-noun rule supplies a compromise. 'numike' concerns negotiation before an outcome, while 'nawo' (agree) does not imply that anyone yielded part of what they wanted.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Communities building shared futures need language for settlements that preserve cooperation while still exposing unequal costs and unresolved needs.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Compromise is available as one outcome of dialogue without being treated as morally required, inherently fair, or preferable to a clear refusal.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Long-lived communal arrangements often depend on revisable settlements among people who must continue living and working together after disagreement.

semantic_domains.communication: an outcome of negotiating differing aims
semantic_domains.community: a settlement reached through concessions among participants
semantic_domains.wisdom: a practical settlement whose fairness still needs examination
